journal_entry.description: Deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue cannot be recognized until performance obligations are satisfied. Since the moving services will be performed in one month, the $5,000 deposit represents a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue) until the services are completed. Cash increases (debit asset) and Unearned Revenue increases (credit liability). The tuples correctly indicate 1 asset increase (debit) and 1 liability increase (credit). No tax is recorded at this point because the taxable event occurs when services are performed, not when the deposit is received.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit via bank transfer, the chequing account (an asset) increases. This is the only debit line in this transaction. The deposit is for future services not yet performed, so it creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) on the credit side, but we only classify the debit side here.

debit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit via bank transfer, the chequing account (an asset) increases. This is the only debit line in this transaction. The deposit is for future services not yet performed, so it creates a liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit) on the credit side, but we only classify the debit side here.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.
